item.title: Kaigake-ike Dam
item.description: Kaigake-ike Dam is an earthfill dam located in Shiga prefecture in Japan. The dam is used for irrigation. The catchment area of the dam is 0.8 km2. The dam impounds about 3 ha of land when full and can store 260 thousand cubic meters of water. The construction of the dam was completed in 1954.[1]

item.title: Amir Bekandeh
item.description: Amir Bekandeh (Persian: اميربكنده)[a] is a village in Hajji Bekandeh-ye Khoshk-e Bijar Rural District[4] of Khoshk-e Bijar District in Rasht County, Gilan province, Iran.

item.title: 35th Combat Aviation Brigade (United States)
item.description: The 35th Combat Aviation Brigade is a subordinate command of the 35th Infantry Division (Missouri Army National Guard), located in Sedalia, Missouri.

item.title: Ivan Vyhovsky
item.description: Ivan Vyhovsky (Ruthenian: Іоан Виговский / Іоан Виговскій / Іоан(ъ) Выговскій / Иоан Виговс(ъ)кий;[2] Ukrainian: Іван Виговський; Polish: Iwan Wyhowski / Jan Wyhowski; date of birth unknown, died 1664), served as hetman of the Zaporozhian Host (1657-1659), leading the Cossack Hetmanate during the Russo-Polish War (1654–1667).

item.title: Gaute Storaas
item.description: Gaute Storaas (born 20 August 1959) is a Norwegian jazz musician (bass) and composer,[1][2] and the older brother of jazz pianist Vigleik Storaas.[3]
